subject: Statistics
title: What Is Regression?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on What Is Regression?.

hook.text: Tall parents tend to have tall kids — but not as tall. Francis Galton called this 'regression to the mean.'

buildup.text: Regression finds the best line through a scatter of points, predicting one variable from another.

discovery.text: The line minimizes the total squared distance from every point. Closest possible fit to the data.

twist.text: Galton's original meaning — things drifting toward average — is different from the modern statistical method.

climax.text: Regression is the backbone of prediction. From economics to medicine, it connects cause to outcome.

punchline.text: Draw a line through the chaos. That's regression.

quiz.question: What does regression do?
options: It finds the best-fitting line through data to predict one variable from another
It proves that one variable causes another
It groups similar data points together
